mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative following month-over-month INDPRO increases in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate sustained above 8.5 percent indicates structurally weak labor demand and depleted household income growth, which forces downward revisions to corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.5% over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Growth in nonfarm payrolls combined with an unemployment rate above seven percent signals early-cycle labor market slack absorption; this joint condition lifts household income and consumption forecasts more strongly than either series alone, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment remained elevated at 9.0% in early 2011 while payrolls showed modest growth
failure_condition: A joint regression of 60-day S&P 500 returns on PAYEMS and UNRATE shows the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) or has a negative sign over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the two years following a business-cycle trough, quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP from a depressed base signals escape velocity that closes the output gap; this reduces recession risk and drives above-average S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:US economy recovering from 2008-2009 recession with GDP still below potential in early 2011
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery windows, quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth fails to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, the largest component of aggregate demand, which directly lifts revenue expectations for consumer-facing corporations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales at 389355 million in April 2011
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 550 thousand units signal a durable recovery in residential construction and household formation, generating positive cyclical spillovers into building materials, employment, and credit markets that lift broad equity indices over the next sixty trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts at 549k in March 2011, near recession lows
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 550k, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year window, or the mean forward return is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices erode corporate profit margins and real household disposable income, prompting expectations of Federal Reserve tightening and raising the equity risk premium, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, an effective federal funds rate above 0.15 percent communicates an earlier-than-expected monetary tightening path, triggering a rapid upward repricing of equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% in April 2011, pinned near zero
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.15%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year window, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).
